title: erysimum × marshallii
intro: Erysimum × marshallii, also known as Marshall's wallflower, is a hybrid plant species in the Brassicaceae family, derived from the cross between Erysimum perenne and Erysimum cheiri. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Erysimum × marshallii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Erysimum × marshallii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
